parsed_review.summary: CP00-405 is a descriptor-only payments-core (RP13) closeout pack covering 40 units (RP13.P06.M03.S13-RP13.P06.M05.S08) across micro-phases M03 (10 rows), M04 (22 rows), M05 (8 rows), following the validated CP392-CP404 pattern. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms full internal consistency: the registry binding, requirements block, manifest, live closeout-pack-plan, and contract projection all agree on pack_id, risk_class B, unit_count 40, range, micro-phase counts (10/22/8), and deliverable distribution (ui:6, claude_review:2, security_audit:10, test:8, implementation:14 = 40, hand-verified against included_units). The service descriptor/case-set emit descriptor-only rows with safe base fields and the freezeCp405Result no-write/no-real-data attestation; validators (coverage + slice) and tests enforce counts, row presence, descriptor-only guards, authority boundary, and contract drift, with the validator script deep-equaling the contract projection against the freshly computed descriptor. The pack-level claim is production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false / implementation_layer:descriptor; the Hermes packet's production_ready_candidate is a clearly distinct descriptor-level flag and emits_hermes_runtime_receipt is false, so Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence, not product runtime. Authority boundaries hold: Claude is read_only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false), human final approval required, and no enterprise-trust claim from local validation. Review hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ blockers (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields), the auth preflight passed with valid JSON, and the validation report shows only Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools and no omitted active files. Changed-file scope is clean: all 7 modified files relate to CP405, index.js is unchanged because wildcard re-exports cover the new functions, and unrelated dirty/untracked files (including the c-ldip-01 invalid-attempt belonging to a different review) are correctly excluded. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
